figures.caption: Fig. 5: Qualitative comparison between PanoSAM2 and other VOS models. The orange boxes highlight the areas where the target object straddles the 0°/360° seam, showing PanoSAM2’s improved segmentation precision in complex scenes at different time steps.
Fig. 12: More ablation visualizations of proposed modules. Orange bounding boxes highlight and zoom in on the small mask region.
Fig. 13: More visual results on self-collected open-world 360 scene.
Fig. 3: Overview of our LSMM framework.

Table 2: Comparisons between our method and existing approaches on PanoVOS validation and test datasets. The best results are shown in bold. Evaluation metric subscript s and u denote scores in seen and unseen categories compared to the training dataset.
Fig. 9: Examples of failure cases. Orange bounding boxes highlight and zoom in on the small mask region.
Fig. 6: Visualization of zero-shot results from PanoSAM2 and other VOS models on the PanoVOS dataset.
Fig. 11: More qualitative comparison between PanoSAM2 and other VOS models. Orange bounding boxes highlight and zoom in on the small mask region.

Table 1: Comparisons between our method and prior arts on the 360VOTS test dataset. Our PanoSAM2 achieves a new state-of-the-art performance. The best results are shown in bold, and * means the method is re-implemented and reproduced.
Fig. 2: Overview of our PanoSAM2 framework. Compared with SAM2 [31], it has two architectural contributions: Pano-Aware (PA) Decoder and Long-Short Memory Module (LSMM).
